description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for learning Indian and International number systems

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of large numbers and notation, can fast-track to both systems with challenge

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of both number systems

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in understanding large numbers or comma usage, needs targeted review

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant review of large numbers and notation before learning number systems

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you're ready to continue with number systems.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: How is 1,00,000 written in International system?
In which system are commas placed every 3 digits?
questions.explanation: International system uses commas every 3 digits (3,3,3). Indian uses 3,2,2,2.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand the differences between both systems.

questions.question: How do you read 5,45,678 in Indian system?
options: Five lakh forty-five thousand six hundred seventy-eight
Five hundred forty-five thousand six hundred seventy-eight
Fifty-four lakh five thousand six hundred seventy-eight
Five thousand four hundred fifty-six seventy-eight
questions.explanation: 5,45,678 in Indian system = Five lakh (5,00,000) + forty-five thousand (45,000) + six hundred seventy-eight (678).

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for crores and larger numbers

ifWrong.message: Practice reading more numbers in both systems before continuing

skip_ahead.action: Skip conversion video, jump to challenge

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on comma patterns

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practicing with parent/teacher reading numbers from newspapers in both systems

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You understand large numbers and notation well. You're ready to learn about Indian and International number systems. We've created a fast-track playlist with challenge content.

good_score.message: Good work! You have the foundation needed. Let's learn how to read and write numbers in both Indian and International systems with complete coverage.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in understanding large numbers or comma usage. Don't worry! We'll start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need.

low_score.message: You need to review place values and large numbers first. We've created a playlist focused on building strong foundation in reading and writing large numbers.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how number system knowledge builds on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 75% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

systemRecognition.description: % of students who can correctly identify which system a number is written in

readingAccuracy.description: % of students who can correctly read numbers in both systems

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 45-50 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (35 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 60-70 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing the comma patterns between the two systems
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasize memory aids: Indian = 3,2,2,2 (first 3, then 2s); International = 3,3,3 (always 3s). Practice identifying which system a number uses
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students mixing terminology (using 'lakh' with International commas)
commonChallenges.solution2: Teach that terminology goes with system: Indian commas → lakhs/crores; International commas → thousands/millions
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students not understanding that both systems represent the same values
commonChallenges.solution3: Show conversions and equivalences repeatedly: 1 lakh = 100,000; 10 lakhs = 1 million. Use real-world examples
